events.place: Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, Japan, Taiwan, China
events.title: Super Typhoon Bavi — Guam & the Northern Mariana Islands, tracking toward Taiwan, Japan & China

ground.text: Bavi's eye passed directly over Rota (Northern Marianas, ~40 mi NE of Guam) at peak intensity — roughly 180 mph (290 km/h) sustained winds, central pressure ~910 mb.
Severe power outages across Guam and Saipan; roads flooded and storm debris left strewn through streets.
Bavi is the third Category-5 storm globally in 2026.

forecast.label: Forecast track
forecast.text: Expected to weaken as it bends west-northwest — over SW Japan's Sakishima Islands (Fri night), near northern Taiwan incl. Taipei (Sat), then eastern China near Shanghai (Sat night) as a tropical storm, driving further heavy rain into an already-flooded region.

articles.title: Super Typhoon Bavi brings destruction to Guam and surrounding Pacific islands

events.title: Deadly flooding across southern China — Hunan under continued-rain watch

ground.text: across Hubei and Guangxi, with thousands displaced, after storms from Tropical Storm Maysak.
(of ~55,000 affected) as of late Mon, 7 Jul.
(Guangxi): the Liulan and Yunbiao reservoirs overtopped and breached; residents call it the worst flooding in about two decades.
Nanning raised its flood-control emergency to the highest level; Xi Jinping called for "all-out" rescue operations.

forecast.label: Why Hunan is flagged
forecast.text: The GDACS footprint sits in Hunan, where forecasters warn of continued heavy rain alongside Guangxi and Guizhou — three provinces home to 150M+ people. Heaviest confirmed impact so far is in Guangxi (Nanning); Hunan's situation is developing.

articles.title: Death toll from China storms and floods rises to 15

Reservoir breaches after extreme rainfall cause severe flooding in Nanning

events.title: Trévillach wildfire, Pyrénées-Orientales — France's most destructive blaze of the year

ground.text: since the fire broke out the evening of 4 Jul near Trévillach, ~35 km from Perpignan — a perimeter of roughly 40 km spanning dozens of communes.
(10,000, then a further 2,000 overnight); two people reported critically injured.
engaged, with around 20 wildfires burning across France.
no longer spreading to new areas and stabilized in places, but not yet fully contained — flanks still active.

articles.title: France wildfire update: Pyrénées-Orientales fire still burning as red alerts raised
~4,500 hectares destroyed, 10,000 evacuated and two critical injuries in the Eastern Pyrenees fire
Thousands Evacuated As Wildfire Burns over 11,000 Acres in Southern France
Wildfire continues to spread in France's Pyrénées-Orientales

events.title: M 5.4 earthquake — Maluku Sea, 162 km NNW of Tobelo, Indonesia

ground.text: A green-alert, offshore quake in the Maluku Sea; independent catalogues log it but note no damage or casualties.
Part of a busy stretch of North Maluku / Halmahera seismicity — a stronger M6.2 struck Halmahera on 3 Jul.

events.no_news: Limited dedicated ground reporting — consistent with a moderate offshore event. Watching for aftershocks.

articles.title: Magnitude 5.4 earthquake strikes near Tobelo, North Maluku, Indonesia
Strong M6.2 earthquake hits Halmahera, Indonesia

events.place: 88 km WNW of Hicks Bay, New Zealand
events.title: M 4.9 earthquake — 88 km WNW of Hicks Bay, off New Zealand's East Cape

ground.text: Around the same period a separate deep M5.4 off Te Kaha was felt across the North Island — from the Bay of Plenty, Gisborne and Hawke's Bay to Wellington and Tauranga — mostly a gentle sway. The East Cape sits above the Hikurangi subduction zone.

events.no_news: The widely-felt shaking in the news refers to the neighbouring M5.4; no distinct ground impact tied to this M4.9.

articles.title: Magnitude 5.4 earthquake strikes off North Island's east coast
A deep magnitude 5.4 earthquake off Te Kaha is felt across the North Island
